name: python-jose (JavaScript Object Signing and Encryption)

auth.notes: Library for implementing JWT auth; no external auth required

performance.notes: Crypto operations via cryptography package; RSA/EC key ops take ~2-10ms

agent_readiness.notes: JOSE (JWT/JWS/JWE/JWK) implementation. from jose import jwt, jwe, jwk, JWTError. token = jwt.encode({'sub': 'user123', 'exp': datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=1)}, key, algorithm='HS256'). claims = jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['HS256']). RSA: jwt.encode(payload, private_key, algorithm='RS256'). JWK: jwk.construct(key_data). JWKS: validate against JSON Web Key Set. FastAPI integration popular. No type annotations — use authlib or PyJWT for better-maintained alternatives.

gotchas: Maintenance is slow — issues and PRs pile up
CVEs have been reported — keep updated and pin version
algorithm='none' vulnerability if algorithms parameter not specified
For FastAPI, python-jose is common but authlib is more actively maintained
best_when: When JWE (encrypted JWT) is needed alongside JWS
JWKS validation for OAuth provider tokens
avoid_when: New projects — prefer authlib or PyJWT for better maintenance
When only simple JWT signing/verification is needed (use PyJWT)
summary: python-jose implements JWT, JWS, JWE, and JWK. jwt.encode(payload, key, 'HS256') / jwt.decode(token, key, ['HS256']). RSA/EC support. JWKS validation. FastAPI-popular JWT library. MIT. Slow maintenance — CVEs have occurred. For new projects prefer authlib (full OAuth/OIDC) or PyJWT (simpler JWT). Always specify algorithms= parameter to prevent algorithm confusion attacks.
